summary.intent: Fix a controlled pagination bug flagged by Codex review on PR #666 in fogodev/ars-ui, then iterate through a second Codex review pass addressing follow-up findings.
summary.outcome: Two rounds of Codex review findings were addressed: the initial fix preserved parent-supplied page values with a clamped effective page, and the corrected contract kept Context::page clamped while using props.page as the controlled source of truth for shrink/expand derivation. Both commits passed all CI gates and were pushed.

repo: ars-ui uses a custom CI gate called `cargo xci-fast` with 10 discrete checks that must all pass before pushing.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment and monitored via GitHub reactions (👀 = active, +1 = approved) and unresolved review threads.

Pagination component in ars-components distinguishes between `Context::page` (always clamped, publicly bindable) and `props.page` (controlled source of truth used internally for shrink/expand decisions).
Proptest suite for pagination uses `--ignored` flag and `PROPTEST_CASES=10000` for the bounds-and-ranges test, suggesting it is expensive and excluded from the default test run.

code.finding: Context::page must be clamped on init, SetPageSize, and SyncProps. SetPageSize must derive new effective page from props.page (controlled prop), not from the already-clamped context value.
Shrink-then-expand regression test was added/updated to cover the case where controlled pagination preserves the parent-supplied page value across page size changes.

workflow: Codex review polling uses a 12-iteration loop with 60-second sleeps, checking reactions and unresolved thread count to detect approval vs. needs-action state.
The flow is: fix → fmt check → focused unit tests → proptest (ignored) → clippy → xci-fast → commit → push → reply to Codex threads and resolve → trigger next @codex review.
GraphQL is used to resolve review threads (resolveReviewThread mutation) and to query unresolved Codex-authored threads; REST is used for reactions and posting comments.
cargo +nightly fmt is run as a separate formatting step after clippy/tests, and a fmt --check diff can appear even after a passing gate run.

friction: Formatting changed one line after all tests passed, requiring an additional `cargo xci-fast` run before the commit — nightly fmt is not idempotent across tool invocations.
Codex polling required up to 12 minutes per pass with manual timeout handling; no native webhook or event-driven notification is available.

The second Codex pass introduced two new threads that required fetching both the thread comments and surrounding source before the correct contract could be understood, adding a full read-then-fix cycle.
open_items: Unit Tests CI job was still pending when the transcript ends — final CI green status not confirmed.
The second corrected commit (`fix(pagination): keep controlled context page clamped`) was not yet replied to on Codex threads and a third review pass had not been triggered by transcript end.
The proptest `--ignored` flag pattern suggests the expensive pagination bounds test is deliberately excluded from normal runs; no indication it will be re-integrated into the default suite.
